name: Request Agent Task
description: Send a request to an agent. PRIMARY tool for agent delegation. Best-effort synchronous: by default waits up to 5 seconds for the agent to finish. If the agent completes in time you get its structured response directly. If not, you get a snapshot {id, status: STARTED, agentId, sessionId} — poll with grid_job_result(id=<that id>, timeout=<ms>) to retrieve the result. Example: agent_request(agentId='Helper', input={task: 'summarise w/notes/daily'}, timeout=30000). For quick jobs set a short timeout; for slow jobs set timeout=0 (pure async) and poll via grid_job_result. For structured output, pass responseSchema.

input.description: The request payload. MUST contain the actual instructions, data, or parameters the agent needs. NEVER pass an empty object {} — the worker has nothing to act on. Use field 'task' for free-form instructions; add other fields the worker needs (data, paths, options). Example: {task: 'summarise w/notes/daily', format: 'bullet list'}.

timeout.description: Milliseconds to wait synchronously for the agent to complete. Default 5000ms. On timeout the response is a STARTED snapshot with the task Job id — poll via grid_job_result to retrieve the final result later. Set to 0 for pure async (snapshot returned immediately).

responseSchema.description: Optional JSON Schema. When provided, the agent's response is constrained to match this schema (server-side enforcement via OpenAI structured outputs). Use to get typed structured output from any agent. Overrides the agent's default output schema if it has one.

sessionId.description: Optional session identifier (hex string). Requests sharing a sessionId are treated as part of the same conversation. If omitted, a new session is created and its id returned in the response.

output.description: Either the agent's structured response (if it completed within the timeout) or a snapshot {id, status: STARTED, agentId, sessionId} to poll via grid_job_result. Always carries the sessionId the task belonged to.
